text.title: WebSocket Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of WebSocket Design. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Client crashes when server adds a new field to payload.
antipattern.fix: Adopt envelope `{v, type, id, ts, payload}`; clients ignore unknown fields under same `v`.

antipattern.symptom: Connection appears alive client-side; server has dropped it 4 minutes ago via NAT timeout.
antipattern.fix: Send ping every 30s; close after 90s no pong; client reconnects.

antipattern.symptom: After server restart, 100k clients reconnect within 200ms; server crashes again.
antipattern.fix: Exponential backoff base 1s max 60s + full jitter; reconnects spread over 60s.

antipattern.symptom: Server OOM after one client stopped reading; metrics showed 50M messages queued.
antipattern.fix: Bound queue at 1000; shed_policy=drop_oldest; emit close code 1008 if shed exceeds threshold.
